messages.content:
Translate

"冒頭の大戸島のシーンで、
呉爾羅が監視等破壊するときに背鰭が微かに揺れるのが着ぐるみのようで懐かしくも奇妙な瞬間。
 #細すぎて伝わらないゴジラマイナスワンの好きなところ選手権"

"In the opening scene on Odo Island, there is a faint sway in the dorsal fin when Gojira destroys the monitoring equipment, which gives a nostalgic yet strange moment reminiscent of a suit. 
#TooSubtleToConveyFavoriteMinorDetailOfGodzillaMinusOneChampionship"

"Yahoo!オークション - セーラームーン 着ぐるみ アトラク コスプレ

衣装タグにBINロゴだからいいやつだ(ぼかした表現)　状態もいいんじゃないんですかね・・・

※ブーツはこうなる"

"Yahoo! Auctions - Sailor Moon Costume Suit Attraction Cosplay

It's a good one because it has the BIN logo on the costume tag (expressed vaguely). The condition is also pretty good, isn't it...

※This is how the boots will be"
"た(きぐるみのすがた)"
"(in the form of a costume/suit)"
"西の果ての長崎で細々と着ぐるみをやっている新参です。家に居る着ぐるみは琥珀(月姫),セイバー,赤セイバー(Fate),涼宮茜(君が望む永遠)明石(艦これ),佐倉杏子(まどか⭐︎マギカ),他のキャラは 5893.jp をご覧ください。ツインエンジェルの公式着ぐるみも撮らせていただきました。"

"I'm a newcomer in the far west of Nagasaki, painstakingly working with costumes. The costumes I have at home are Kohaku (Tsukihime), Saber, Red Saber (Fate), Suzumiya Akane (Kimi ga Nozomu Eien), Akashi (Kantai Collection), Sakura Kyoko (Madoka⭐︎Magica), and for other characters please visit 5893.jp. I've also had the opportunity to photograph the official costume for Twin Angel."
"ここ1ヶ月で初めて着ぐるみ「見た」わ　寝てたけど、、、"
"This past month was the first time I've 'seen' a costume in person. Well, I was asleep, though..."

"うまこちゃんかわいいよね・・・レインボーな可愛さだ

うまこちゃん、かわいいよね・・・"

"Umako-chan is cute, isn't she... her cuteness is like a rainbow.

Umako-chan is really cute..."

"佐賀競馬の公式キャラ「さがのうまこ」ちゃんです
佐賀競馬が現在地に移転してから50周年記念ということで「50」のポーズなんだとか
そしてなんとこの子は今日新馬戦・・・じゃなかった、デビューだそうで！これからよろしくお願いします！
#さがけいば #うまてなし"

"This is 'Saga-no-Umako-chan,' the official character of Saga Racecourse. It seems that her '50' pose is in celebration of the 50th anniversary since the Saga Racecourse moved to its current location. And what's more, this little one is in her first race today... oops, I mean her debut! Please give her your support from now on!
#SagaRacecourse #HorseAppeal"

Translate to Japanese

Bike racing costumed character
バイクレースの着ぐるみキャラクター

Translate

"バイクレース 着ぐるみ"
"Bike race costume"

Translate to Japanese

Yuru-chara
ゆるキャラ
Bike racing stadium
バイクレース場

Translate

"Yuru-chara at the bike racing stadium"

Translate to Japanese

Yuru-chara for bike racing stadium
バイクレース場のゆるキャラ
"beautiful girl costume"
美少女のコスチューム

Translate

"美少女の着ぐるみ"
"Beautiful girl costume"

Translate
"紧急事态！演员受伤！接下来的握手会，就决定是你上场了！后勤君！"
"Emergency situation! The actor is injured! For the next handshake event, it's decided that you're going on stage! Logistics-kun!"

"アニメの方はそれほどヒットしたわけではないのに（失敬な！）ショーはいい出来でした。特にキャラクター造型のレベルが高く、公演回数が少なかったのが惜しまれるショーのひとつです。

主人公ジュディのペットのオウムが登場するのですが、手に乗せる形にしてセリフをしゃべる時には手で動かして動きを与えていました。｢どれみ｣ショーのマジョリカといい、｢ジャンヌ｣ショーのフィンといい、こういう演出は今も昔も変わらないようで。"

"Despite the anime not being a huge hit (no offense!), the show was well-produced. In particular, the high level of character design is noteworthy, and it's regrettable that there weren't more performances – it's one of those shows you wish had more run time.

The protagonist Judy's pet parrot makes an appearance, and when it speaks its lines, it’s perched on the hand and given movement by the hand. This sort of production, like Majolica in the 'DoReMi' show or Finn in the 'Jeanne' show, seems to be an unchanging staple from the past to present."

Translate

피치메모리인형탈
Peach memory mascot costume

Translate to Korean

"girl mascot costume"
여자 마스코트 탈 (yeoja maskeot tal)

Translate to Korean

Princess
공주
What is the etymology of "인형탈"?

The Korean term "인형탈" (inhyeongtal) is composed of two parts: "인형" (inhyeong) and "탈" (tal).

1. "인형" (inhyeong) means "doll" or "puppet," derived from the Sino-Korean word:
   - "인" (in) comes from the Chinese character "人" meaning "person" or "human."
   - "형" (hyeong) comes from the Chinese character "形" meaning "shape" or "form."

2. "탈" (tal), in this context, refers to a "mask" or "headgear," especially that worn by a performer in a play or traditional Korean masked dances known as "탈춤" (talchum). The word "탈" is also of Sino-Korean origin.

Put together, "인형탈" literally translates to something like "doll mask" or "puppet mask" and refers to a costume headpiece or entire outfit that represents a character, often worn by mascot performers. It can also be associated with life-sized mascot suits commonly seen at promotional events, theme parks, or sports games.
